overview.importance: Shri Mahakaleshwar is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas and the spiritual centre of a Ujjain journey. The south-facing form of Shiva is revered across India, drawing pilgrims for darshan, worship and the distinctive Bhasma Aarti tradition.
overview.centrality: Mahakal is closely connected with the old city, Harsiddhi Mata, Ram Ghat and the Mahakal Lok corridor. Planning it first makes the rest of the Ujjain temple circuit easier to pace.
overview.duration: Keep 2–4 hours for a normal visit, and a larger buffer on weekends, festivals and Kumbh crowd days. Timings should be verified from official temple sources before travel.

darshanTypes.title: Normal Darshan
darshanTypes.description: The standard queue-based darshan option for most visitors. Crowd flow, entry gates and waiting time can change.

darshanTypes.title: Bhasma Aarti
darshanTypes.description: A revered pre-dawn ritual that generally requires advance availability checks, identity verification and specific rules.

darshanTypes.title: VIP / Paid Darshan
darshanTypes.description: Official assisted or paid categories may be offered subject to current temple policy and availability.

darshanTypes.title: Festival Crowd Days
darshanTypes.description: Expect route changes, longer queues, security controls and restricted vehicle access around the temple area.

bhasmaSteps.title: Check availability
bhasmaSteps.description: Use only official Mahakaleshwar temple sources to understand the current process.

bhasmaSteps.title: Book in advance
bhasmaSteps.description: High-demand dates can fill quickly. Avoid unofficial agents or guaranteed-entry claims.

bhasmaSteps.title: Carry original ID
bhasmaSteps.description: Keep the same valid identification used during the official booking process.

bhasmaSteps.title: Reach early
bhasmaSteps.description: Allow time for security, verification, footwear and route changes near the temple.

bhasmaSteps.title: Follow dress code and rules
bhasmaSteps.description: Reconfirm clothing, bag, phone and entry requirements before leaving your hotel.

nearbyTemples.name: Kal Bhairav
nearbyTemples.subtitle: Guardian deity of Ujjain
nearbyTemples.description: An ancient and distinctive shrine associated with the guardian form of Shiva.
nearbyTemples.familyTip: Suitable for families; prepare children for busy ritual activity.

nearbyTemples.name: Harsiddhi Mata
nearbyTemples.subtitle: Historic Shakti temple
nearbyTemples.description: A revered temple known for its tall Maratha-era lamp towers and evening atmosphere.
nearbyTemples.familyTip: Easy to combine with Mahakal, Mahakal Lok and Ram Ghat.

nearbyTemples.name: Mangalnath
nearbyTemples.subtitle: Temple associated with Mars
nearbyTemples.description: A peaceful Shipra-side temple important in Ujjain’s astrological traditions.
nearbyTemples.familyTip: A calmer stop with open surroundings and easier pacing.

nearbyTemples.name: Chintaman Ganesh
nearbyTemples.subtitle: Ancient Ganesh temple
nearbyTemples.description: A popular old temple where devotees seek blessings before important journeys and decisions.
nearbyTemples.familyTip: Family-friendly, though access time should include the drive from central Ujjain.

nearbyTemples.name: Sandipani Ashram
nearbyTemples.subtitle: Traditional place of learning
nearbyTemples.description: Associated with the education of Shri Krishna, Balarama and Sudama.
nearbyTemples.familyTip: A meaningful, quieter educational stop for children and grandparents.

nearbyTemples.name: Ram Ghat
nearbyTemples.subtitle: Sacred Shipra riverfront
nearbyTemples.description: Ujjain’s principal pilgrimage ghat and an atmospheric place for aarti and a riverside pause.
nearbyTemples.familyTip: Keep children close near steps and water; elders may need assistance.

familyTips.title: Avoid peak heat
familyTips.description: Use early morning and late afternoon for outdoor temple travel.

familyTips.title: Keep water available
familyTips.description: Carry drinking water and any regular medicines in a light day bag.

familyTips.title: Plan rest breaks
familyTips.description: Return to the hotel or sit in a calm area after a major queue.

familyTips.title: Limit temple count
familyTips.description: Four well-paced stops are better than an exhausting all-day checklist.

familyTips.title: Prefer early darshan
familyTips.description: Earlier starts often provide cooler weather and a calmer family rhythm.

plans.title: Full-Day Temple Circuit
steps: Harsiddhi and Ram Ghat in the evening

faqs.question: How much time is needed for Mahakal Darshan?
faqs.answer: Keep 2–4 hours for a normal visit and more on weekends, festivals or Kumbh crowd days. Timings and queue arrangements should be verified from official temple sources before travel.

faqs.question: Is Bhasma Aarti suitable for families?
faqs.answer: It can be meaningful for prepared families, but the very early reporting, waiting, dress rules and crowd conditions may be tiring for young children or some elderly visitors.

faqs.question: What are the main nearby temples?
faqs.answer: Popular additions include Harsiddhi Mata, Kal Bhairav, Mangalnath, Chintaman Ganesh, Sandipani Ashram and Ram Ghat.

faqs.question: Can elderly visitors comfortably visit?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a lighter itinerary, early starts, planned rest, minimal luggage and current assisted-access guidance from official temple sources.

faqs.question: What should be avoided during peak crowd?
faqs.answer: Avoid tight onward connections, too many temples in one day, unofficial agents, heavy bags and separating from your group without a fixed meeting point.
